symptoms.symptom_name: Postural tachycardia with pre-syncope, settling on lying flat

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Exaggerated postural HR rise with pre-syncope, relieved by recumbency, is the POTS phenotype.
Strictly postural tachycardia that settles lying flat is against a primary arrhythmia, but ECG is still needed.

symptoms.symptom_name: No random palpitations at rest lying flat

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of rest palpitations lowers arrhythmia probability, but does not exclude paroxysmal arrhythmia without ECG/Holter.

symptoms.symptom_name: Near-syncope without true loss of consciousness

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Pre-syncope without true LOC is less concerning than exertional/sudden syncope, but fall risk remains high.

symptoms.symptom_name: No chest pain, normal oxygen, racing settles lying flat

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No chest pain/hypoxia and immediate settling on recumbency argue against PE.
Fatigue and orthostatic intolerance are core post-COVID dysautonomia features.
Maintained BP and moist mucosa argue against severe volume depletion as the cause.

symptoms.symptom_name: Postural vitals 72→128 bpm with BP maintained; regular rhythm; intact neuro (examination)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A >30 bpm postural HR rise without orthostatic hypotension is the diagnostic hallmark of POTS.
Absence of orthostatic hypotension argues against volume depletion.

investigation_details.expected_result: Expected sinus tachycardia on standing without arrhythmia; required to exclude a primary arrhythmia.
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Cannot exclude a paroxysmal arrhythmia without ECG/Holter.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: ECG/Holter is required to exclude an arrhythmic cause of the tachycardia/pre-syncope.
Post-viral onset is a well-recognised trigger for POTS/dysautonomia.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: A sustained postural HR rise (>30 bpm) with pre-syncope, relieved by recumbency, no rest palpitations, adequate hydration and BP maintained, in a post-viral patient, is the POTS phenotype — but an arrhythmia cannot be excluded without ECG, and the fall risk is high, so a prompt in-person review with ECG plus fall-safety measures is required.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: To exclude an arrhythmic cause of tachycardia/pre-syncope.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Active stand test / formal postural vitals
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: To document the postural HR rise objectively; a supervised home stand test can support this.
To exclude anaemia/thyroid causes and plan autonomic assessment.
